'''Andrew John Northey''' (born 17 February 1972) is an English former [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. In rugby league he played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[Super League]] as a {{rlp|sr}} before returning to rugby union in 1997, and joining [[Northampton Saints]].

==Career==
Born in [[St Helens, Merseyside|St Helens]], Northey started his career as a [[rugby union]] player at [[Waterloo F.C.|Waterloo]]. In December 1994, he switched codes to play for his home town [[rugby league]] club [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Northey changes codes to join St Helens |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/northey-changes-codes-to-join-st-helens-1390472.html |website=The Independent |access-date=13 June 2022 |date=22 December 1994}}</ref>

Northey played for St. Helens at {{rlp|ce}} in their [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final victory over the [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|accessdate=3 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|archive-date=8 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Northey appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|ce}} [[Anthony Sullivan (rugby)|Anthony Sullivan]] on 47-minutes) in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]' 16-25 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1995–96 League Cup (rugby league)|1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1995–96 Rugby Football League season|1995–96]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 13 January 1996.<ref name="13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007005311/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|archive-date=7 October 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref>

At Northampton he was a replacement in the victorious [[2000 Heineken Cup Final]] as they defeated [[Munster Rugby|Munster]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/sport/rugby_union/766699.stm|title=Saints secure historic victory|publisher= [[BBC]]|date=27 May 2000|access-date=7 September 2014}}</ref> He joined [[Rotherham R.U.F.C.|Rotherham Titans]] in 2000 for the tail-end of their meteoric rise through the leagues but a knee injury curtailed his playing career. He joined the club's community department as a rugby development officer whilst running the Under-18s, and was offered the chance to take control of first team affairs following the departure of [[Mike Schmid (rugby union)|Mike Schmid]] in 2004.

Northey was a popular figure at Rotherham Titans because of his sharp wit, no-nonsense approach and stories of the 'good old days' of rugby league but was never considered a serious contender to lead the club on a permanent basis. Following the appointment of [[Andre Bester]] as Head Coach, Northey was offered the chance to become Assistant Coach but opted to take a similar role at [[Sedgley Park R.U.F.C.]]

As of 2010, Northey coached [[Macclesfield R.U.F.C.]] in [[National Division One]] with [[Geoff Wappet]].

==Personal life==
Northey is the son of the rugby league {{rlp|ce}} for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]; [[Keith Northey]],<ref name="Profile at saints.org.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.saints.org.uk/saints.org.uk/home/viewpage.php?page_id=10&num=16066|title=Profile at saints.org.uk|publisher=saints.org.uk|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref> and the grandson of the [[association football]]er ([[Manchester United F.C.|Manchester United]] ([[Reserve team]]), [[Prescot Cables F.C.|Prescot Cables]] and [[St Helens & District Football Combination|Haydock Villa]]), and [[Coach (sport)|coach]] ([[St Helens Town A.F.C.|St. Helens Town]]); [[Bob Northey (footballer)|Bob Northey]].<ref name="Keith Northey Profile at saints.org.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.saints.org.uk/saints.org.uk/home/viewpage.php?page_id=10&num=16067|title=Keith Northey Profile at saints.org.uk|publisher=saints.org.uk|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref>
